meaning: adj.脊骨的,脊髓的n.脊椎麻醉
examples: This movement lengthens your spine and tones the spinal nerves.
Almost all sensory information from the trunk and limbs passes through the spinal cord.
Repairs to the nervous system will start with optic nerves and, in time, the spinal cord.
Prof James said end-stage prostate cancer attacks the bones and could lead to spinal cord compression and paralysis.
Prosecutors say Kermit Gosnell, 72, killed babies by using scissors to cut their spinal cords.
